title: Financial assets
description: Test your knowledge about different types of financial assets.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following financial assets is the most liquid?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Which of the following best defines liquidity?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The ability to convert a store of value to cash
Uncertainty about the value of an asset
The amount that will be repaid at the end of a bond’s term
Investing in several different assets with unrelated risks
The expenses of negotiating and executing an exchange

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best defines liquidity?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The ability to convert a store of value to cash
Uncertainty about the value of an asset
The amount that will be repaid at the end of a bond’s term
Investing in several different assets with unrelated risks
The expenses of negotiating and executing an exchange

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is a paper claim that entitles the buyer of that claim to future income from the seller?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

**Which of the following best defines the risk of a financial asset?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The uncertainty that an asset might gain or lose value
The probability that an asset will lose value
The amount that will be repaid at the end of a bond’s term
Investing in several different assets with unrelated risks
The expenses of negotiating and executing a trade of that asset

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is a paper claim that entitles the buyer of that claim to future income from the seller?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Which of the following best defines the risk of a financial asset?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The uncertainty that an asset might gain or lose value
The probability that an asset will lose value
The amount that will be repaid at the end of a bond’s term
Investing in several different assets with unrelated risks
The expenses of negotiating and executing a trade of that asset

choices.content: The uncertainty that an asset might gain or lose value

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is a paper claim that entitles the buyer of that claim to future income from the seller?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Which of the following financial assets carries the most risk?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**All else equal, which of the following financial assets carries the most risk?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Several years ago the Golden Company issued bonds to raise funds so that it could buy equipment. Those bonds were purchased by the Bank of Westeros. However, now the Bank of Westeros has decided that it doesn’t want to have any assets in the form of bonds, so it is selling off all the bonds that it owns. 

**Which of the following is most likely to be the result of this action?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The default risk of the bonds will increase.
The opportunity cost of holding money will decrease.
The face value of the bonds will decrease.

assessment_items.question_content:
Daenerys bought government bonds with a face value of $\$1000$ last year. At the time, she could have earned $6\%$ interest if she had deposited money in a savings account at the Iron Bank. All of a sudden, interest rates at the Iron Bank increased to $9\%$. 

**What will happen to the price of Daenerys’s bonds?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The price of the bonds will increase
The price of the bonds will decrease
The price of the bond will become the price that makes the interest earned on both assets equal
The price of the bond will remain $\$1000$
The price of bonds is unaffected by the interest rate

assessment_items.question_content:
Maxistan’s central bank lowered interest rates last month.

**All else equal, what would we expect to happen to previously issued bonds in Maxistan?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The par value of bonds will increase
Nothing will change because bonds are unaffected by interest rates

assessment_items.question_content:
**What happens to the price and interest rate of a bond if the demand for that bond increases?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Price increases; interest rate is unaffected.
Price is unaffected; interest rate is unaffected.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What happens to the price and interest rate of a bond if the demand for that bond increases?**



[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Price increases; interest rate is unaffected.
Price is unaffected; interest rate is unaffected.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What happens to the price and interest rate of a bond if the demand for that bond increases?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Price increases; interest rate is unaffected.
Price is unaffected; interest rate is unaffected.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following *best* describes the cost of money?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**If the interest rate on bonds increases, which of the following is the most likely result?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: People will be willing to hold less wealth in the form of money.
The transaction costs associated with holding money will increase.
The quantity demanded of money increases

choices.content: People will be willing to hold less wealth in the form of money.
